Title: Multi-Dimensional Space-Time Multilevel Codes
Authors: Philippa A. Martin, David M. Rankin, Desmond P. Taylor
Abstract: To date there has been little work done on multilevel codes for the space-time environment. In this paper we develop multi-dimensional space-time multilevel codes (ST-MLCs). Several construction methods are proposed, including a coset code approach. The proposed space-time multilevel encoders partition a 2Nt-dimensional signalling space, which spans all Nt transmit antennas. The multi-dimensional ...

Title: A 0.18-$muhbox m$CMOS Analog Min-Sum Iterative Decoder for a (32,8) Low-Density Parity-Check (LDPC) Code
Authors: S. Hemati, A.H. Banihashemi, C. Plett
Abstract: Current-mode circuits are presented for implementing analog min-sum (MS) iterative decoders. These decoders are used to efficiently decode the best known error correcting codes such as low-density parity-check (LDPC) codes and turbo codes. The proposed circuits are devised based on current mirrors, and thus, in any fabrication technology that accurate current mirrors can be designed, analog MS dec...

Title: A Turbo-Decoding Message-Passing Algorithm for Sparse Parity-Check Matrix Codes
Authors: M.M. Mansour
Abstract: A turbo-decoding message-passing (TDMP) algorithm for sparse parity-check matrix (SPCM) codes such as low-density parity-check, repeat-accumulate, and turbo-like codes is presented. Title: A Note on “Signal-to-Noise Ratio Mismatch for Low-Density Parity-Check Coded Magnetic Recording Channels” by W. Tan and J. R. Cruz
Authors: H. Saeedi, A.H. Banihashemi
Abstract: In this letter, we prove that the minimum signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) threshold of the iterative belief propagation algorithm for a low-density parity-check (LDPC) coded magnetic recording channel is obtained when the SNR mismatch factor is half the ratio of variance to mean for the Gaussian distribution at the channel output. This result was first observed by Tan and Cruz empirically

Title: Weight Distribution of Low-Density Parity-Check Codes
Authors: C. Di, T.J. Richardson, R.L. Urbanke
Abstract: We derive the average weight distribution function and its asymptotic growth rate for low-density parity-check (LDPC) code ensembles. We show that the growth rate of the minimum distance of LDPC codes depends only on the degree distribution pair. It turns out that capacity-achieving sequences of standard (unstructured) LDPC codes under iterative decoding over the binary erasure channel (BEC) known...

Title: An Ensemble of Iteratively Decodable Codes Constructed Based on a Superposition Method
Authors: Telex Magloire Ngatched Nkouatchah, Fambirai Takawira
Abstract: In this paper, the construction of new families of error-correcting codes adapted from Boutros and Lentmaier's generalized low-density (GLD) codes, which we called generalized irregular low-density (GILD) codes, is investigated. By introducing irregular base matrices, significant improvements on Boutros and Lentmaier's results are achieved. Two cases are considered; in the first case, the same con...

Title: Average Coset Weight Distribution of Combined LDPC Matrix Ensembles
Authors: T. Wadayama
Abstract: In this paper, the average coset weight distribution (ACWD) of structured ensembles of low-density parity-check (LDPC) matrices, which are called combined ensembles, is discussed. A combined ensemble is composed of a set of simpler ensembles such as a regular bipartite ensemble. Two classes of combined ensembles have prime importance; a stacked ensemble and a concatenated ensemble. The ACWD formul...

Title: Hardware efficient decoding of LDPC codes using partial-min algorithms
Authors: J.-n. Su, K. Liu, H. Min
Abstract: In this paper, the partial-min algorithms are proposed for updating the check node messages in decoding low-density parity-check (LDPC) codes. The proposed methods can achieve a good performance-versus-complexity tradeoff by reducing both the check node processor complexity and the decoder memory requirements. Float-point and fixed-point simulation results show that a near optimum performance can ...

Title: On the Stopping Redundancy of Reed–Muller Codes
Authors: T. Etzion
Abstract: The stopping redundancy of the code is an important parameter which arises from analyzing the performance of a linear code under iterative decoding on a binary erasure channel. In this paper, we will consider the stopping redundancy of Reed-Muller codes and related codes. Let R(lscr,m) be the Reed-Muller code of length 2m and order lscr. Schwartz and Vardy gave a recursive construction of parity-c...

Title: Consensus Propagation
Authors: C.C. Moallemi, B. Van Roy
Abstract: We propose consensus propagation, an asynchronous distributed protocol for averaging numbers across a network. We establish convergence, characterize the convergence rate for regular graphs, and demonstrate that the protocol exhibits better scaling properties than pairwise averaging, an alternative that has received much recent attention. Title: The Universal Operation of LDPC Codes Over Scalar Fading Channels
LDPC codes based on circulant permutation matrices for fast encoding
Authors: Yizhou Ma, Moonho Lee, Yang Xiao
Abstract: In this paper we present a class of algebraically structured quasi-cyclic (QC) low-density parity-check (LDPC) codes which have lower encoding complexity than randomly constructed LDPC codes for the specific structure of their parity-check matrices. Since the parity-check matrices are comprised of blocks of circulant permutation matrices, the storage efficiency can be improved significantly. We al...

Title: An improved PEG construction of irregular LDPC codes by conditioning low-degree nodes
Authors: Hua Chen, Zhigang Cao
Abstract: Progressive Edge-Growth (PEG) algorithm is an important approach to construct good LDPC codes at short block lengths. PEG construction with optimized symbol-node degree distributions yield irregular codes with very good performance, especially in the water-fall region. In this paper, we present a modification to the PEG algorithm by conditioning low-degree symbol nodes in the Tanner graph. Our mod...

Title: Lower bounds of weights and distances of good LDPC codes
Authors: Yang Xiao, Moon Ho Lee
Abstract: Since the distance computation is a NP problem, most presented evaluations of LDPC codes are mainly based on the girth numbers of the codes. The computation for the distance of the codes is also depended on the girth numbers. There has been no simple way to obtain the distance of LDPC codes because the code length is very long. Title: A design of LDPC codes with large girth based on the sub-matrix shifting
Authors: Jun Fan, Yang Xiao
Abstract: In this paper, we present an approach for constructing LDPC codes with girth 8. First, we design 3 submatrices with different shifting functions given by our schemes, then combine them into a matrix according to our approach, finally, expand the matrix into a desired parity-check matrix using identity matrices and cyclic shift matrices of the identity matrices. Title: Construction of good quasi-cyclic LDPC codes
Authors: Yang Xiao, Moon Ho Lee
Abstract: The BER performance of presented quasi-cyclic (QC) low-density parity-check (LDPC) codes is not good as that of randomly constructed LDPC codes, while the lack of structure of randomly constructed LDPC codes implies serious disadvantages in terms of storing and accessing a large parity-check matrix. To solve the problems, this paper proposes a design of good quasi-cyclic (QC) low-density parity-ch...

Title: Design of variable-rate adaptive LDPC codes for DVB-S2 applications
Authors: Nishit Pandya, Bahram Honary
Abstract: Both constant and variable Forward Error Correcting (FEC) coding is provided by the DVB-S2 system[ 11]. Variable FEC can be used to provide different levels of error protection according to applications, & may be combined with the use of return channels, to achieve Adaptive Coding. Title: Performance of LDPC-Coded interleave-division multiple-access (IDMA) system
Authors: Jun Ling, Lin Wang, Yao Luo, Haibin Wang
Abstract: In this paper regular (3,6) LDPC codes is applied to IDMA system (called as LDPC-Coded IDMA system) and performance comparison between IDMA systems based on LDPC Codes and based on convolutional codes is completed over different transmitting environments without power attribution. Simulation results show that the system purposed by this paper owns excellent capacity of multiple access similar to t...

Title: Robust error correction technique using time-frequency interleave LDPC product code for MC-CDMA system
Authors: S. Wada, Z. Liu, T. Baba
Abstract: In this paper, a robust error correction technique is developed for the Multi-Carrier Code Division Multiple Access (MC-CDMA) system to improve the quality of communication. The technique is based on Time-Frequency Interleave (TFI) Low Density Parity Check (LDPC) product code in consideration of error propagation. Title: Generalized quasi-cyclic LDPC codes for wireless data transmission
Authors: Abdi Moinian, Bahram Honary, Ernst Gabidulin
Abstract: While in wired access networks meeting packet error requirements requested by the applications are simplified by the stability of the channel conditions, in the case of wireless networks, this is not the case. Title: Differential acquisition of m-sequence using sum-product iterative decoding method
Authors: Zhi-song Bie, Kai Niu, Li Zhang, Wei-ling Wu
Abstract: Sum-product algorithm has been considered as near-optimal decoding algorithm for LDPC-like codes. In this paper, sum-product algorithm is used to acquisition of m-sequences. And a special property of m-sequences is utilized to implement incoherent differential acquisition for single path channel. Title: Implementation of fountain codes over fading channels
Authors: Bo Yang, Rolando Carrasco, Alan Adams
Abstract: Fountain codes are a class of rateless codes. In this paper, the design and construction of Luby Transform (LT) codes, the first efficient class of Fountain Codes with fast encoding and decoding algorithms, is presented with new simulation results evaluating their performance over the Rayleigh fading channel. Title: An efficient decoder for turbo product codes built using two-error-correcting eBCH codes
Authors: Guotai Chen, Lei Cao, Lun Yu, Changwen Chen
Abstract: In this paper, we propose a method to reduce the number of test patterns (TPs) decoded in the Chase II algorithm when turbo-decoding product codes are constructed with two-error-correcting extended Bose-Chaudhuri-Hocqenghem (eBCH) codes. Title: Object-oriented unequal loss protection with product codes for wireless video transmission
Authors: Zhen Qiu, Takeshi Ikenaga, Satoshi Goto
Abstract: This paper presents an object-oriented unequal loss protection scheme for robust wireless scalable 3D-SPIHT encoded video transmission using product codes. In our proposed OOULP system, the embedded video bitstream of all concerned video objects are packetized independently for transmission over wireless network. The scheme employs regular LDPC codes and Reed-Solomon codes to deal effectively with...

Title: Exploiting Irregular Variable Node Degree in a MIMO System
Authors: Mohamed R Ismail
Abstract: Low density parity check (LDPC) codes have shown excellent performance in error correction capability rivaling and even surpassing turbo codes. Multiple-input-multiple-output (MIMO) antenna technology has demonstrated the means of delivering increased diversity gain and increased throughput. Title: Reduced Complexity Decoding Algorithm of LDPC Codes using Node Elimination
Authors: Joon-sung Kim, Hong-yeop Song
Abstract: We propose a modified belief propagation algorithm that uses node elimination for bit nodes of large log-likelihood ratio. With this algorithm, we can reduce decoding complexity and improve performance by enhancing the cycle properties. Simulation results show that the decoding complexity is reduced and the proposed algorithm outperforms the belief propagation algorithm in the high-Eb/No region

Title: A simple algorithm to design irregular LDPC codes for finite length
Authors: Mehrdad Taki, Mahdi Orooji
Abstract: A simple and practical algorithm to design an irregular low density parity check (LDPC) codes for finite length applications is introduced. The simulation result will be proved the advantages of designed codes in compare with conventional regular and irregular finite length LDPC codes. Title: Decoding of Quasi-cyclic LDPC Codes Using an On-the-Fly Computation
Authors: Kiran K. Gunnam, Gwan S. Choi, Weihuang Wang, Euncheol Kim, Mark B. Yeary
Abstract: The implementation complexity of the decoder for low-density parity-check codes (LDPC) is dictated by memory and interconnect requirements. Authors: Don Torrieri, Avinash Mathur, Amitav Mukherjee, Hyuck M. Kwon
Abstract: This paper proposes a scheme of iteratively obtaining an estimate of channel coefficient and noise power spectral density (PSD), initially with the aid of pilot symbols. The iterative receiver employs a low-density parity-check (LDPC) code and channel estimation based on expectation maximization. Title: Direct Space-Time GF(q) LDPC Modulation
Authors: Adam R. Margetts, Keith W. Forsythe, Daniel W. Bliss
Abstract: Wireless communication using multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems improves throughput and enhances reliability for a given total transmit power. In this paper, potential performance gains are investigated via a direct Galois field [GF(q)] low-density parity-check (LDPC) space-time coding and modulation scheme. Authors: Alexandre de Baynast, Arnab Chakrabarti, Ashutosh Sabharwal, Behnaam Aazhang
Abstract: We address the problem of low density parity-check (LDPC) code design for relay channel in time-division mode based on a distributed strategy: In the first time slot, the source transmits part of the codeword. Title: Hard Decision Error Correcting Scheme Based on LDPC Codes over Impulse Noise Channels
Authors: Milos Ivkovic, Shuguang Cui
Abstract: We investigate hard decision based low-density parity-check (LDPC) codes over an impulse noise channel, which is modeled as the concatenation of a Binary Erasure Channel (BEC) and a Binary Symmetric Channel (BSC). Title: Structured Interleavers and Decoder Architectures for Zigzag Codes
Authors: Tejas Bhatt, Victor Stolpman
Abstract: We propose structured interleaver design for parallel concatenated Zigzag codes. While the proposed design performs as good as or better than random interleavers for various block-sizes, it improves the error floor of the Zigzag codes and offers a lot of parallelism suitable for high data-rate applications. Title: Iterative Joint Detection and Decoding for MIMO-OFDM Wireless Communications
Authors: Keun Chul Hwang, Sungwoo Park, Moon June, Soon Young Yoon
Abstract: In this paper, we propose a simplified iterative joint detection and decoding (IJDD) scheme for MIMO-OFDM communications, which reduces the extremely high computational complexity of conventional iterative joint detection and decoding receiver with negligible performance degradation. For the MIMO detection, we propose a simplified maximum likelihood detection (MLD), which reduce the complexity of ...

Title: Iterative Extended Soft-RLS Algorithm for Joint Channel and Frequency Offset Estimation for Coded MIMO-OFDM Systems
Authors: Kyeong Jin Kim, Tejas Bhatt, Ronald A. Iltis
Abstract: In this paper, we propose an iterative extended soft- RLS (IES-RLS) algorithm for a joint channel and frequency offset estimation for the coded MIMO-OFDM system. In this system, the a posteriori probability for the information bit computed from the channel decoder is used in the MIMO data detector, whose coded soft symbol decision is used in the proposed IES-RLS algorithm. Title: Carrier and Timing Synchronization of BPSK via LDPC Code Feedback
Authors: Esteban L. Valles, Richard D. Wesel, John D. Villasenor, Christopher R. Jones
Abstract: In traditional receiver architectures, symbol acquisition and tracking are performed using phase lock techniques that are independent of the channel-code decoding process. In [1] feedback from the constraint-node side of a bi-partite graph is used to estimate symbol frequency and timing offset in a baseband pilotless transmission. Title: Frame Error Rate Analysis of Coded MIMO Systems with Spatial Multiplexing
Authors: Mikko Vehkapera, Markku Juntti
Abstract: Recently, El Gamal and Hammons (2001) proposed a simple method for deriving noise thresholds that fully determine the asymptotic error probability behavior of certain type of error correcting codes. (2006) to derive analytical lower bounds for the frame error rate of coded multi-antenna systems utilizing spati...

Title: Variable-Rate Low-Density Check Codes for DVB-S2 Applications
Authors: Bahram Honary, Nishit Pandya
Abstract: Both constant and variable forward error correcting (FEC) coding is provided by the DVB-S2 system. Title: The Coding-Spreading Trade-off in LDPC-Coded Interleave-Division Multiple-Access (IDMA) System
Authors: Yao Luo, Lin Wang, Jun Ling
Abstract: In this paper capacity-achieving low-density parity-check (LDPC) codes is applied to IDMA System, called as, LDPC-Coded IDMA system, in which the associated coding- spreading tradeoff problem is mainly investigated. Title: Performance Advantage of Non-binary LDPC Codes At High Code Rate under AWGN Channel
Authors: Long Ma, Lin Wang, Jianwen Zhang
Abstract: Two different decoding algorithms for non-binary LDPC codes: the fast Fourier transform belief propagation (FFT-BP) decoding algorithm and the Log-domain belief propagation (Log-BP) decoding algorithm are introduced in this paper. Their performance advantage of two different decoding algorithms for LDPC Codes over GF(q) is investigated at high code rates (1/2~1) over AWGN channel. The performance ...

Authors: Wei Zhou, Ai-dong Men, Li-Ye Zhao, Zi-yi Quan
Abstract: Based on decoding performance, complexity and latency of finite-geometry low-density parity-check (LDPC) codes, an efficient decoding algorithm is proposed. The algorithm is a hybrid bit-flipping (BF)/majority-logic (MLG) algorithm. In the algorithm, there are two stages. At the first stage, a code is decoded with bit-flipping (BF) with a small fixed number of iterations. Then the output from bit-...

Title: Enhanced HARQ Employing LDPC Coded Constellation Rearrangement with 64QAM
Authors: Min-li Jia, Jing-ming Kuang, Zun-wen He
Abstract: In this paper, a novel algorithm for searching optimal HARQ methods with constellation rearrangement is proposed. This novel method tries to average the combined reliability of demodulated bits after retransmissions. Title: Burst Error Studies with DVB-S2 and 3D Wavelet Reversible Variable-Length Coding for Ultraspectral Sounder Data Compression
Authors: Bormin Huang, Alok Ahuja, Y. Sriraja
Abstract: Satellite data transmission may possess random and burst errors. Our previous study [1] shows that 3D wavelet transform with reversible variable-length coding (3 DWT- RVLC) [1] has significantly better error resilience than JPEG 2000 Part 2 [2,3] on 1-bit random error remaining after channel decoding. In this paper, we investigate the burst error correction performance of the Digital Video Broadca...

Title: LDPC Coded Multicarrier CDMA for Future Communications
Authors: Luan yingzi
Abstract: In this paper, we describe the basic concept of LDPC and MC-CDMA, and compare the coded and uncoded MC-CDMA system. Using iterative decoding and multiuser detection, the system performance will be improved.

Authors: Cai Yueming, Xu Xin, Cheng Yunpeng, Xu Youyun, Li Zi
Abstract: In this paper, the Probabilistic Data Association (PDA) detection method for Multiple-Input Multiple-Output (MIMO) detection in V-BLAST systems is extended to the Soft-In-Soft-Out (SISO) iterative signal detection problem between PDA detector and channel decoder. Title: Optimized Rotations for LDPC-coded Gray-mapped MPSK Constellations with Signal Space Diversity
Authors: Nauman F. Kiyani, Umar H. Rizvi, Jos H. Weber, Gerard J. M. Janssen
Abstract: Rotation of the signal constellation together with in-phase and quadrature channel interleaving (signal space diversity) is known to provide good performance gains over fading channels for multi level modulation methods. Title: LDPC DECODING ALGORITHM WITH ESTIMATION OF NOISE VARIANCE
Authors: Changzheng Ma, Boon Poh Ng
Abstract: As the code length approaches infinite, LDPC codes have the potential of approaching Shannon limits. The original message passing (sum-product) algorithms for AWGN channel require the knowledge of noise variance. If the assumed noise variance deviates from the true value, the performance tumbled. Title: A Method of Counting the Number of Cycles in LDPC Codes
Authors: Jun Fan, Yang Xiao
Abstract: For a given block length sequences of the underlying Tanner graph (TG), the short circles of low-density parity check (LDPC) codes can have considerable variation in performance. By analyzing the shapes of the cycles of TG in parity check matrix, this paper presents a method of counting the number of 4-cycles, 6-cycles, 8-cycles and 10-cycles. Title: Evaluations of Good LDPC Codes Based on Generator Matrices
Authors: Yang Xiao
Abstract: Since the weight and distance computations of LDPC codes is a NP problem, most presented evaluations of LDPC codes are based on the girth numbers of the codes, and the computation for the distance of the codes is also depended on the girth numbers. Title: Expurgated Bound Estimation of LDPC Codes
Authors: Yang Xiao, Moon Ho Lee
Abstract: The expurgated bound is proposed to evaluate good LDPC codes in this paper. To get the expurgated bound for LDPC codes needs to know the weights' distribution of LDPC codes to be evaluated, while the calculation of the weights' distribution is NP problem. Title: Adaptive LDPC Decoding for Multipath Channels
Authors: Yang Xiao, Moon Ho Lee
Abstract: Presented low-density parity-check (LDPC) decoding exhibits poor BER performance for multipath channel (MPC). To solve the problem, this paper proposes an adaptive LDPC decoding system, which composes two parts: adaptive equalizer and LDPC decoder. Title: Advantages of Product Accumulate Codes over Regular LDPC Codes underAWGN Channel
Authors: Min Xiao, Lin Wang, Weikai Xu, Haibin Wang
Abstract: Product accumulate (PA) codes are a class of codes with good performance, low complexity, regular structure and flexible rate adaptation, which include PA-I codes and PA-II codes. Title: Very Large Girth Column-weight two Quasi-cyclic LDPC Codes
Authors: Gabofetswe Malema, Michael Liebelt
Abstract: In this paper a modified bit-filling algorithm is used for constructing column-weight two quasi-cyclic low-density parity-check codes with large girths. Code rows are divided into groups. Title: Encrypting LDPC-Codec
Authors: Yang Xiao, Ying Zhao, Moon Ho Lee
Abstract: The presented LDPC-codec has no encrypting function since its rediscovering, and the presented encrypting for signal transmission needs additional algorithm and program, which leads the great time delay for the signal receiving. Title: Information Hiding Scheme Based on LDPC coding
Authors: Lingjun Kong, Yang Xiao
Abstract: This paper proposes a new information hiding scheme in images via LDPC coding. Generally, resisting the bits attacking is nearly impossible for the conventional information hiding system. In this article low-density parity check codes are introduced into the information hiding system because of the better performance of their own. Title: A Robust Anti-collusion Coding in Digital Fingerprinting System
Authors: Jie Yang, Xiaoxia Xu
Abstract: A novel robust anti-collusion encoding scheme which combines the balanced incomplete design (BIBD) code with the turbo code in digital fingerprinting system is proposed. The fingerprints are coded by turbo code before embedding and then added to the wavelet domain which is suitable for the human vision. Title: Recent Results on Capacity-Achieving Codes for the Erasure Channel with Bounded Complexity
Authors: Igal Sason, Henry D. Pfister
Abstract: The paper introduces ensembles of accumulate-repeat-accumulate (ARA) codes which asymptotically achieve capacity on the binary erasure channel (BEC) with bounded complexity (per information bit). It also introduces symmetry properties which play a central role in the construction of various capacity-achieving ensembles for the BEC. Title: On Extrinsic Information of Good Codes Operating Over Memoryless Channels with Incremental Noisiness
Authors: M. Peleg, A. Sanderovich, S. Shamai
Abstract: We show that the Extrinsic Information about the coded bits of any good (capacity achieving) code operating over a binary symmetric channel is zero when the code operates at code rate above capacity and a positive constant otherwise, that is, the Extrinsic Information Transfer (EXIT) chart is a step function of channel quality, for any capacity achieving code. Title: On the Error Exponents of Improved Tangential-Sphere Bounds
Authors: Moshe Twitto, Igal Sason
Abstract: The performance of maximum-likelihood (ML) decoded binary linear block codes over the AWGN channel is addressed via the tangential-sphere bound (TSB) and two of its recent improved versions. Title: Bit loading for OFDM with Quasi-constant transmission power
Authors: Elad Ben Izhak, Ofer Amrani
Abstract: This work addresses the problem of adaptive bit loading typically employed for approaching the capacity of a fading channel. Title: The Effect of Signal Distortion Techniques for PAPR Reduction on the BER Performance of LDPC and Turbo Coded OFDM System
Authors: Referendo D. Soriano, Joel S. Marciano
Abstract: Signal distortion techniques namely, peak windowing and clipping, are methods to address the high peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR) problem on OFDM systems. However, signal distortion technique introduces in-band and out-of-band distortion which may undermine the gain given by an error control code. Title: A comparison of Time domain equalizers on MIMO frequency selective channels
Authors: Yusuke Goto, Yasunori Iwanami, Eiji Okamoto
Abstract: MIMO (multiple input multiple output) techniques which use multiple antennas for both transmitter and receiver are now very important for high speed wireless communications. Title: Adaptive Hybrid ARQ schemes with bit-LLR based packet combining through MIMO-OFDM eigen-mode channels
Authors: Hirotake Ishigami, Mayumi Shimotsu, Yasunori Iwanami, Eiji Okamoto
Abstract: In this paper, we consider the hybrid ARQ schemes with the adaptive modulation and the bit-LLR based packet combining through MIMO-OFDM eigen-mode channels for high throughput transmission.
